risky_cuts.reason: Revenue-critical feature (subscriptions) referenced in business flows cannot be deferred.
Handling subscriptions and refunds is revenue-critical and carries high risk if omitted.
Account registration is a fundamental prerequisite for user access and all subsequent business flows.
Password reset is a security-essential basic function required for account recovery.

over_kept.reason: Gamification features (streaks and achievements) are not in critical business flows and provide low MVP value.
Notification management is a medium-frequency basic feature not tied to core business flows.

weak_rationale.issue: Rationale states 'frequency=medium' and 'strategy=aggressive' which contradicts the CORE decision, as identical rationales resulted in DEFER for other tasks.
Relies solely on a blanket 'high frequency' justification, ignoring that it is a basic category feature not in a business flow.
Rationale only cites high frequency, failing to document its critical necessity within the core business flow.

competitive_risks.risk: Feature being cut despite having no competitor coverage, potentially missing opportunity for viral growth
Feature being cut despite having no competitor coverage, losing potential personalization advantage
Critical monetization feature deferred while all competitors have it, risking revenue loss
Core user onboarding feature deferred while all competitors have it, creating barrier to user acquisition

differentiators.opportunity: Unique content creation capability that no competitor offers
Quality control feature that differentiates content quality from competitors
Unique analytics dashboard for monitoring learning progress
Innovative quality assessment feature for AI interactions
Comprehensive user management system not available in competitor products
